lifecycles._note: Secret Vault CRUD covering the 2 uncovered WRITE ops: POST /v1/secretvault (create vault) and PUT /v1/secretvault/{secret_vault_id}/terminated (schedule deletion). Bodies from data/api_docs.json (secretvaultcreaterequest / secretvaultterminatedrequest) — _UNPROVEN (no api_bodies.json entry, no live 2xx). Field rules from the doc schema: name MUST match ^[a-z0-9]*$ len 3-63 (so 'regrsv{unique}' -> use lowercase-alnum {ualpha}/{unique}); temporary_key_ttl_nhours in [1,36]; vault_token_ttl_ndays in [30,7300]; acl_cidr is a comma-separated CIDR string; access_key_id is a REQUIRED real Access Key ID (the SCP_ACCESS_KEY of the caller) — we send a placeholder doc value, so create is OPTIONAL and likely 4xx (invalid/foreign access key) but still RECORDS the POST /v1/secretvault write. Create captures $.secret_vault.id (doc envelope secretvaultcreateresponse.secret_vault -> secretvaultkey.id). If create succeeds, the lifecycle reads it (probe-reads exercises GET /v1/secretvault/{secret_vault_id} + GET /v1/temporarykey/{secret_vault_id}) then schedules termination via PUT .../terminated (waiting_time_ndays in [7,30]); the cleanup also issues the terminate (the only teardown verb — there is no hard DELETE on secretvault). The terminate step is optional+grouped so when create did not yield an id it still fires against a {placeholder} id, recording the PUT .../terminated write and 4xx-ing. All guarded so a missing/foreign access key isolates as a warning, not a run failure.
